Internal Compliance Memorandum — Reg FD and Privacy Review re: NorthStar TheraBank, Inc. (U.S. issuer; integrated healthcare–banking operations).
Background: The Company is a Delaware corporation listed on a U.S. national securities exchange and subject to Reg FD and HIPAA/GLBA obligations.
On 2026-05-28, Ms. Mira Velas (age 42, DOB 1983-11-02), Senior Director, Clinical Billing, West Carillon, CA office, user "mvelas", device NS‑TB‑IMAC‑042, IP 10.18.7.43, badge NS‑09477, joined call sess:c2‑7b via https://meet.nstb.local/r/earnings?mtg=Q3a-19%2F.
During a prep with Mr. Dalton Crowe (sell-side analyst, Pineroot Securities LLC), she referenced preliminary Q2 payer mix shifts and unaudited claim denial rates not yet disclosed in an 8‑K.
She also cited an internal dashboard showing a 3.2% variance in capitated revenue at the CardiaLumen device program and a covenant headroom estimate tied to the Revolving Credit Facility.
While no earnings update has been released, management has not determined a material adverse change (no MAC) as of this memo, and no forward-looking statements should be made beyond the Q1 public script.
Portions of the call drew on public-source data (Federal Health Data Portal aggregates and state dashboards), which alone would not be MNPI when used without internal overlays.
However, selective disclosure of internal claims cadence and trial milestone timing to a covered market actor creates Rule 10b‑5 and Reg FD risk and requires broad dissemination if material.
Separately, an anonymized log included 05/12/1989, ZIP 30303, and serial ZX‑8891, but these were unlinked fields and do not identify a consumer, household, or patient.
A redacted finance export referenced a c c t: 2 6 - 7 1 - x x 9 and card ending ••4411 without names, and such generic demographics should not be treated as PII on their own.
For clarity, we do not collect Social Security numbers in the analyst portal, device MAC addresses are not stored, and all patient identifiers are removed per policy PR‑17.
Note that Ms. Velas works from the West Carillon, CA office and uses the N S - T E A M S iOS app on NS‑iPhone‑27, which increases re-identification risk if combined with her other operational identifiers.
Any future briefings during the quiet period must route through IR, include at least one public webcast, and avoid sharing account-level metrics, IP logs, or session IDs with analysts.
For questions contact legal@northstar-therabank.com or the Compliance Line at 877‑555‑0194; do not email spreadsheets containing patient rows to external domains.
Action items: issue a corrective Reg FD 8‑K if needed, retrain employees on the Analyst Interaction SOP, and document this incident in EthicGate case EG‑2026‑41.
